name: Run Playwright Tests in GitHub Actions
description: Configure Playwright to run E2E tests in GitHub Actions CI with browser caching, parallel execution, artifact uploads, and failure reporting.
trendingReason: CI integration is essential as teams adopt Playwright for production test suites

steps.title: Create the GitHub Actions workflow
steps.description: Set up a workflow file for running Playwright tests on push and PR.
steps.command:
mkdir -p .github/workflows && cat > .github/workflows/playwright.yml << 'EOF'
name: Playwright Tests
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  test:
    timeout-minutes: 30
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npx playwright install --with-deps chromium
      - run: npx playwright test --project=chromium
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
        with:
          name: playwright-report
          path: playwright-report/
          retention-days: 14
EOF

steps.title: Configure CI-specific settings
steps.tip: The 'github' reporter annotates failed tests directly in the PR checks tab. Use 'forbidOnly' to fail CI if .only() is accidentally committed.

steps.title: Add browser caching
steps.description: Cache Playwright browsers to speed up CI runs.
steps.command:
echo 'Add this step before playwright install in the workflow:

- uses: actions/cache@v4
  with:
    path: ~/.cache/ms-playwright
    key: playwright-${{ hashFiles("package-lock.json") }}'

steps.title: Run the workflow locally for testing
steps.description: Test the CI pipeline locally before pushing.
steps.tip: Setting CI=true locally simulates the CI environment including retries and reporter settings.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Playwright CI Setup
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Creating GitHub Actions Workflow ==="
mkdir -p .github/workflows

cat > .github/workflows/playwright.yml << 'WORKFLOW'
name: Playwright Tests
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  test:
    timeout-minutes: 30
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Cache Playwright browsers
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/ms-playwright
          key: playwright-${{ hashFiles('package-lock.json') }}

      - name: Install Playwright browsers
        run: npx playwright install --with-deps chromium

      - name: Run Playwright tests
        run: npx playwright test --project=chromium

      - name: Upload test report
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
        with:
          name: playwright-report
          path: playwright-report/
          retention-days: 14
WORKFLOW

echo ""
echo "=== Workflow created at .github/workflows/playwright.yml ==="
echo ""
echo "=== Local CI Simulation ==="
echo "Run: CI=true npx playwright test --project=chromium"

faq.question: How do I run tests against a deployed preview URL?
faq.answer: Pass the URL via environment variable: 'PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL=https://preview-123.example.com npx playwright test'. In GitHub Actions, use the deployment_status event or extract the preview URL from your deploy step output.

faq.question: Why should I install only one browser in CI?
faq.answer: Installing all three browsers adds 500+ MB to each CI run. Most bugs are caught with a single browser. Run Chromium in CI and test Firefox/WebKit periodically or in a separate job to save time and cost.

faq.question: How do I debug a test that only fails in CI?
faq.answer: Enable trace recording with 'trace: on-first-retry' in your config. Download the trace artifact from the CI run and open it with 'npx playwright show-trace trace.zip'. The trace includes screenshots, DOM snapshots, network logs, and console output.
